annotations.label: Fundamental theorem of algebra

anchor.snippet: every non- constant single-variable polynomial with complex coefficients has at least one complex root

annotations.note: Direct statement: every complex polynomial of positive degree has a root.

annotations.label: Algebraic closure of C (equivalent form)

anchor.snippet: the field of complex numbers is algebraically closed

annotations.note: The instance `Complex.isAlgClosed : IsAlgClosed ℂ` states exactly this.

annotations.label: Counting roots with multiplicity

anchor.snippet: every non-zero, single-variable, degree n polynomial with complex coefficients has, counted with multiplicity , exactly n complex roots

annotations.note: Combined with `IsAlgClosed.splits`, this gives that a complex polynomial's `roots` multiset has cardinality equal to its degree.

annotations.label: Real-coefficient FTA equivalent

anchor.snippet: Every univariate polynomial of positive degree with real coefficients has at least one complex root

annotations.note: Specialising `IsAlgClosed.exists_aeval_eq_zero` to `ℂ` with a real polynomial yields a complex root.

annotations.label: Complex-coefficient FTA equivalent

anchor.snippet: Every univariate polynomial of positive degree with complex coefficients has at least one complex root

annotations.note: Same as the lead statement.

annotations.label: Linear factorization over C

anchor.snippet: Every univariate polynomial of positive degree n with complex coefficients can be factorized

annotations.note: Together with `IsAlgClosed.splits` for ℂ, this gives the product-of-linear-factors decomposition.

anchors.snippet: Every univariate polynomial of positive degree n with complex coefficients can be factorized

annotations.label: Multiplicity of a root

anchor.snippet: the number of its occurrences is, by definition, the multiplicity of the root

annotations.note: Defined as the multiplicity of `X - C a` dividing `p`.

anchors.snippet: the number of its occurrences is, by definition, the multiplicity of the root

annotations.label: Real quadratic factor exists

anchor.snippet: Every univariate polynomial with real coefficients of degree larger than two has a factor of degree two with real coefficients

annotations.note: Mathlib proves irreducible real polynomials have degree ≤ 2 but does not package the corollary that a higher-degree polynomial has a real quadratic divisor.

annotations.label: Factorization into linear and quadratic real factors

anchor.snippet: Every univariate polynomial with real coefficients of positive degree can be factored

annotations.note: The irreducible-factor classification is there, but the explicit ∏ linear · ∏ quadratic factorization theorem for ℝ[X] is not packaged.

anchors.snippet: Every univariate polynomial with real coefficients of positive degree can be factored

annotations.label: Growth lemma for polynomials

anchor.snippet: a polynomial function p ( z ) of degree n whose dominant coefficient is 1 behaves like z n when | z | is large enough

annotations.note: States `‖eval z p‖ → ∞` as `‖z‖ → ∞` for `0 < degree p`.

annotations.label: Real-analytic divisibility by quadratic

anchor.snippet: a real-valued polynomial p ( x ): p (0) ≠ 0 of degree n > 2 can always be divided by some quadratic polynomial with real coefficients

annotations.note: Mathlib has divisibility by a real quadratic given a non-real complex root, but not the article's standalone divisibility statement under `p(0) ≠ 0`.

annotations.label: Axler's proof via De Moivre and EVT

anchor.snippet: Sheldon Axler has a proof of the fundamental theorem of algebra using De Moivre's formula and extreme value theorem on a compact set

annotations.note: Mathlib's `Complex.exists_root` is proved via Liouville, not Axler's EVT/De-Moivre argument.

annotations.label: Proof via maximum modulus principle

anchor.snippet: The maximum modulus principle applied to 1/ p ( z ) implies that p ( z 0 ) = 0

annotations.note: Mathlib's FTA proof does not go via the maximum modulus principle applied to 1/p.

annotations.label: Proof via Liouville's theorem

anchor.snippet: Applying Liouville's theorem , which states that a bounded entire function must be constant

annotations.note: This is exactly the proof of `Complex.exists_root`, invoking Liouville's theorem on 1/p.

annotations.label: Proof via argument principle

anchor.snippet: Yet another analytic proof uses the argument principle

annotations.note: The argument principle is not in Mathlib, so this proof is unavailable.

annotations.label: Equivalence with eigenvalue existence

anchor.snippet: it suffices to show that every complex square matrix of size n > 0 has a (complex) eigenvalue

annotations.note: Mathlib's existence of an eigenvalue over `IsAlgClosed K` specialises to ℂ, giving the matrix-eigenvalue equivalent.

annotations.label: Proof via Rouché's theorem

anchor.snippet: Rouché's theorem gives perhaps the shortest proof of the theorem

annotations.note: Rouché's theorem is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Topological proof via minimum of |p|

anchor.snippet: Suppose the minimum of | p ( z )| on the whole complex plane is achieved at z 0

annotations.note: Mathlib does not contain this topological proof of FTA.

annotations.label: Topological proof via winding number

anchor.snippet: For another topological proof by contradiction, suppose that the polynomial p ( z ) has no roots

annotations.note: Winding numbers are not formalised in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Analytic facts used in algebraic proofs

anchor.snippet: every polynomial with an odd degree and real coefficients has some real root

annotations.note: The abstract real-closed-field axiom is stated, but no `IsRealClosed ℝ` instance is registered to specialise it to ℝ.

annotations.label: Real-closed field extension is algebraically closed

anchor.snippet: if R is any real-closed field , then its extension C = R ( √ −1 ) is algebraically closed

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `IsRealClosed` but does not prove `IsAlgClosed (R[√-1])` from it.

annotations.label: Algebraic proof by induction on 2-adic valuation

anchor.snippet: This statement can be proved by induction on the greatest non-negative integer k such that 2 k divides the degree n

annotations.note: Mathlib does not contain the 2-adic-valuation algebraic proof of FTA.

annotations.label: Shipman's improvement (prime degree suffices)

anchor.snippet: any field in which polynomials of prime degree have roots is algebraically closed

annotations.note: Shipman's prime-degree-suffices theorem is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Galois-theoretic proof

anchor.snippet: Another algebraic proof of the fundamental theorem can be given using Galois theory

annotations.note: Mathlib's `Complex.isAlgClosed` is proved via Liouville, not via Galois theory.

annotations.label: Flat Riemannian surface

anchor.snippet: A Riemannian surface ( M , g ) is said to be flat if its Gaussian curvature

annotations.note: Gaussian curvature and flat Riemannian surfaces are not formalised in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Geometric proof via Gauss-Bonnet

anchor.snippet: the existence of a non-constant polynomial p ( z ) without zeros implies the existence of a flat Riemannian metric over the sphere

annotations.note: The Gauss-Bonnet theorem and this geometric proof of FTA are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: C is the algebraic closure of R

anchor.snippet: The field of complex numbers is the algebraic closure of the field of real numbers

annotations.note: The two ingredients (`Complex.isAlgClosed` and `finrank_real_complex` giving algebraicity) exist, but no explicit `IsAlgClosure ℝ ℂ` instance was found.

annotations.label: Complex linear factorization corollary

anchor.snippet: Every polynomial in one variable z with complex coefficients is the product of a complex constant and polynomials of the form z + a with a complex

annotations.note: Combined with `IsAlgClosed.splits` at ℂ, this gives the constant-times-product-of-linear-factors decomposition.

annotations.label: Real factorization into linear and irreducible quadratic factors

anchor.snippet: Every polynomial in one variable x with real coefficients can be uniquely written as the product of a constant

annotations.note: Irreducible real polynomials are known to have degree ≤ 2, but the explicit unique-factorization statement into linear and irreducible quadratic factors is not packaged.

annotations.label: Partial fraction decomposition over R

anchor.snippet: Every rational function in one variable x , with real coefficients, can be written as the sum of a polynomial function

annotations.note: Partial-fraction decomposition over ℝ does not appear in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Elementary primitive of rational functions

anchor.snippet: every rational function in one variable and real coefficients has an elementary primitive

annotations.note: The notion of elementary primitives of rational functions is not formalised.

annotations.label: Algebraic extensions of R

anchor.snippet: Every algebraic extension of the real field is isomorphic either to the real field or to the complex field

annotations.note: Exactly states that an algebraic ℝ-extension is `ℝ`-isomorphic to either ℝ or ℂ.

annotations.label: A priori modulus bound on zeros

anchor.section: Bounds on the zeros of a polynomial
anchor.snippet: all zeros ζ of a monic polynomial

annotations.note: Cauchy's bound (1 + max |a_i|/|a_n|) is formalised as `cauchyBound` and bounds the norm of any root.

annotations.label: p-norm bound on zeros

anchor.section: Bounds on the zeros of a polynomial
anchor.snippet: a bound can be given directly in terms of any p-norm of the n -vector of coefficients

annotations.note: Only the Cauchy bound is formalised; no p-norm-based root bound was found.
